body:
Issue Type: Performance / Reliability
Area: Infrastructure

Problem Statement
AgentWatch's auditor model is a single point of failure. When the primary model provider has:

API outages (OpenAI downtime)

Rate limiting spikes

Unexpected latency > 10 seconds

Model deprecation (e.g., GPT-3.5 turbo retirement)

The entire safety system degrades or fails. No automatic fallback, no graceful degradation.

Proposed Solution
Build a production-grade model router with sophisticated fallback and load shedding:

Provider-agnostic router supporting:

OpenAI (GPT-4o, GPT-4, GPT-3.5)

Anthropic (Claude 3.5, 3)

Google (Gemini Pro, Flash)

[Together.ai](https://together.ai/) / Fireworks (open-source models)

Local models (Ollama, vLLM, llama.cpp)

Intelligent routing strategies:

Latency-based: Route to fastest responding provider

Cost-optimized: Use cheaper models for low-risk steps

Quality-tiered: High-risk actions use strongest model

Round-robin: Load balance across multiple keys

Fallback orchestration:

text
Primary (GPT-4o) ΓåÆ timeout 3s ΓåÆ fallback (Claude) ΓåÆ timeout 5s ΓåÆ fallback (Gemini) ΓåÆ timeout 10s ΓåÆ degrade (local Llama) ΓåÆ timeout 15s ΓåÆ fail open (allow but log)
Smart circuit breakers:

Track error rates per provider/key

Open circuit after 5 failures in 60 seconds

Half-open after 30 seconds with probe request

Auto-heal when success rate recovers

Cost-aware load shedding:

Real-time token counting per session

Budget alerts at 80%, hard stop at 100%

Dynamic model selection: downgrade to cheaper model when budget < 20%

Queue requests when rate limit approaching (leaky bucket)

Performance metrics:

P95, P99 latency per provider

Cost per successful audit

Provider reliability scores (SLA tracking)

Acceptance Criteria
Router supports 6+ providers with unified interface

Automatic failover within 500ms of primary failure

Circuit breaker prevents cascading failures

Cost tracking within 5% accuracy across all providers

Zero downtime during provider outages (proven with chaos testing: kill OpenAI API)

Dashboard shows real-time router health, cost, and routing decisions

Configuration via YAML with hot reload

95%+ test coverage including network partition simulations

Technical Notes
Use tenacity for retry logic with exponential backoff

Implement provider-specific rate limit parsers (different headers)

Store routing metrics in Prometheus (existing Grafana integration)

Integration point: ModelRouter class in agentwatch/infrastructure/router.py

Consider using litellm as base library but fork for custom routing

Additional Considerations
Cache layer: Cache responses for identical prompts (with TTL based on risk level)

Request coalescing: Deduplicate identical concurrent requests

Priority queue: High-risk actions skip queue

Health checks: Background threads testing provider endpoints every 30s
title: [Feat] :Zero-Downtime Model Router with Fallback Orchestration and Cost-Aware Load Shedding

body:
Issue Type: Feature
Area: Causal Memory / Storage

Problem Statement
AgentWatch tracks sessions but lacks a queryable graph of causal relationships across sessions and agents. Questions that cannot be answered today:

"Show me all hallucinations that originated from a model update 3 days ago"

"Which actions in Session B were influenced by Session A's decisions?"

"What would have happened if step 12 was approved instead of blocked?"

Proposed Solution
Build a directed causal graph stored in a graph database (Neo4j or PostgreSQL + pgvector) with time-travel query capabilities:

Causal node types:

Decision - agent action or auditor block

State - system snapshot after action

Prompt - user or system prompt

Memory - retrieved from cross-session memory

Failure - detected hallucination or block

Edge types with weights:

CAUSES - direct causation (action A led to state B)

INFLUENCES - weak correlation

COUNTERFACTUALLY_DEPENDS_ON - alternative histories

Time-travel query language:

sql
-- Find all failures caused by high confidence scores > 0.9
FROM causal_graph
MATCH (f:Failure)-[:CAUSED_BY]->(a:Action)
WHERE a.agent_confidence > 0.9
AND f.timestamp > NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'
RETURN f, a, a.agent_confidence
ORDER BY f.timestamp

-- Counterfactual: what if step 5 was different?
COUNTERFACTUAL SESSION 'abc-123'
MODIFY STEP 5 SET tool_args = '--dry-run'
THEN TRACE STEP 6..20
Cross-session causal inference:

Detect when Session B's failures correlate with Session A's actions

Build "contamination tracking" (bad memory poisoning good sessions)

Suggest causality with confidence scores (p-values, correlation coefficients)

Storage optimization:

Graph pruning after 30 days (keep nodes, summarize edges)

Compressed storage for repeated patterns

Shard by time window for performance

Acceptance Criteria
Graph database integration with support for 10k+ nodes per day

TQL (Time-travel Query Language) with 10+ core query patterns

Counterfactual replay works for sessions up to 50 steps

Cross-session causal detection identifies at least 3 real causal patterns from historical data

Query latency < 500ms for graph traversals up to depth 10

Dashboard includes causal graph explorer with D3.js visualization

REST endpoints for all TQL operations

Technical Notes
Neo4j preferred for native graph queries, but consider SQL+pgvector for simpler deployments

TQL can be a DSL or use existing query language with macros

Integration point: CausalMemory class in agentwatch/memory/causal.py

Counterfactual requires checkpoint system (existing rollback infrastructure)

Related Issues
#234 (existing mini level 10 - coordinate with that effort)
title: [Feat] :Causal Memory Graph with Time-Travel Query Language
